sources.commercial_conflict_note: Lauren Aloise is co-founder of Devour Tours, a commercial food tour operator active in Spain. ⚠ coi
sources.author_byline_note: Author known from site context (Lauren Aloise is sole author of spanishsabores.com); byline not visible in article body

restaurants_raw.name: Perromedio
restaurants_raw.quote: Visiting Perromedio is not just going out to eat or dine: it is living a gastronomic experience that connects past and present. The restaurant combines the authenticity of recipes like rabo de toro with careful service and a privileged setting in the city center.

restaurants_raw.name: Kiosko Las Titas
restaurants_raw.quote: Art Nouveau style establishment facing the Genil River, offering Mediterranean tradition with Granada touches. Suitable for quiet afternoons and evening dining, with a varied menu of quality ingredients meant for sharing.

restaurants_raw.name: La Barra de San Remo
restaurants_raw.address_hint: Acera del Casino / Fuente de las Batallas
restaurants_raw.quote: Newer version of the classic San Remo, run by Aitor Pozuelo, characterised by neighbourhood tradition, flavour, and familiarity. Informal, ideal for extended afternoons sharing tapas and conversation.

restaurants_raw.name: Taberna Mítico Bar
restaurants_raw.address_hint: Near Plaza Derecho / Placeta del Colegio
restaurants_raw.quote: Established venue with an aura of the classic — ese aura de clásico — featuring outdoor terrace seating and gastronomy rooted in traditional preparation methods.
restaurants_raw._quality_flag: low-confidence: quote lacks specific dish detail

restaurants_raw.name: Bar Avila Tapas
restaurants_raw.cuisine: Spanish tapas (free tapa with each drink)
restaurants_raw.quote: Jamón asado (slow roast pork) described as crispy on the outside, moist in the middle.

restaurants_raw.name: Bodegas Castañeda
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Rabo de toro; house cocktail calicasas
restaurants_raw.quote: Tortilla and potato salad, rabo de toro (oxtail stew with peppers and potatoes), jamón, and the house cocktail calicasas.

restaurants_raw.name: Bar Los Diamantes
restaurants_raw.quote: Fried fish specialties; free tapa with each drink at the lively original Calle Navas location.
restaurants_raw._quality_flag: low-confidence: short quote from primary — reinforced by secondary source

restaurants_raw.name: Café 4 Gatos
restaurants_raw.quote: Toast with tomato, manchego, jamón, coffee, and fresh orange juice — the perfect Albaicín breakfast stop.

restaurants_raw.name: Gran Café Bib-Rambla
restaurants_raw.quote: Historic café established in 1907; churros with chocolate, a Granada institution.

restaurants_raw.name: Rincón de Rodri
restaurants_raw.quote: Grilled chiperones (baby squid).

restaurants_raw.name: Los Manueles
restaurants_raw.quote: Giant albondigas (meatballs) at this Granada institution open since 1917.

restaurants_raw.name: Bar Futbol
restaurants_raw.quote: Churros with chocolate.

restaurants_raw.name: Nito's Pub
restaurants_raw.quote: Blue and Manhattan cocktails.
restaurants_raw._quality_flag: TIER-C: bar only (no food), under 15 words

restaurants_raw.name: Tajine Elvira
restaurants_raw.quote: Couscous, fragrant tagines, and pastela at this Albayzín Moroccan restaurant, one of the most popular dining spots in the neighbourhood.

restaurants_raw.name: La Trastienda
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Cured meats and artisanal cheeses with wine
restaurants_raw.quote: Cured meats, artisanal cheeses, and carefully selected wines in a cosy space at Plaza de Cuchilleros.

restaurants_raw.name: Taberna Catavinos
restaurants_raw.quote: Baby squid and generous raciones at this neighbourhood Albayzín taberna.

restaurants_raw.name: La Botillería
restaurants_raw.quote: Good wine selection, homemade desserts, and meat-focused cooking at the Realejo border.

restaurants_raw.name: Casa de Vinos La Brujidera
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Local wines; tapas improving with each round
restaurants_raw.quote: Local wines, with tapas improving in quality and size as the evening progresses.

restaurants_raw.name: Taberna La Tana
restaurants_raw.quote: Local Granada wines.
restaurants_raw._quality_flag: TIER-C: secondary source with only 1 specific detail

restaurants_raw.name: Om Kalsum
restaurants_raw.quote: Moroccan-inspired free tapa with each drink.
restaurants_raw._quality_flag: TIER-C: secondary source with only 1 specific detail

restaurants_raw.name: El Trillo
restaurants_raw.quote: Beautifully presented dishes in a quiet corner of the Albaicín.
restaurants_raw._quality_flag: TIER-C: secondary source with 0 specific dish details

restaurants_raw.name: Heladería Los Italianos
restaurants_raw.quote: Italian-style gelato including a Granada-specific pionono flavour.
restaurants_raw._quality_flag: TIER-C: not a restaurant (gelateria/ice cream only)

restaurants_raw.name: Pastelería López-Mezquita
restaurants_raw.quote: The home of Granada's famous piononos.
restaurants_raw._quality_flag: TIER-C: not a restaurant (pastry shop without table service)

restaurants_raw.name: Gran Café Bib-Rambla
restaurants_raw.quote: Freshly fried churros at this long-established café on Plaza de Bib-Rambla.
restaurants_raw._not_restaurant_flag: café only — appears in 2 sources (James Dimitri + Spanish Sabores) → TIER-A but recommended removal

restaurants_raw.name: Café 4 Gatos
restaurants_raw.quote: Enormous and delicious toasts in a hidden Albaicín placeta.
restaurants_raw._merge_note: Same as Café 4 Gatos from src-james-dimitri

restaurants_raw.name: Restaurante Más Que Vinos
restaurants_raw.quote: Top-ingredient tapas and artisanal cheese in the city centre.
restaurants_raw._quality_flag: AUTO-REMOVED by concentration cap: lowest-confidence single-source Tier B from Spanish Sabores

restaurants_raw.name: El Mercader
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Lebanese-inspired lamb with mint and couscous
restaurants_raw.quote: Lebanese-inspired lamb subtly flavored with mint, served with couscous and white yogurt; followed by a spiced orange cake with white chocolate sauce.

restaurants_raw.name: Pimienta Rosa
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Arroz seco de presa ibérica; fried eggplant with honey
restaurants_raw.quote: Arroz seco de presa ibérica (Andalusian rice with Iberian pork and artichokes); fried eggplant with honey; white chocolate and cauliflower croquettes.

restaurants_raw.name: Betula Nana
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Ajo blanco (cold almond-garlic soup with anchovies)
restaurants_raw.quote: Ajo blanco — cold almond-garlic soup served with anchovies — at this 18-cover contemporary seafood gastrobar.

restaurants_raw.name: Bar Avila Tapas
restaurants_raw.signature_dish: Jamón asado (slow roast pork); pollo asado
restaurants_raw.quote: Croquetas, bocadillos, and pollo asado (roast chicken with salt, herbs, and juices) — no reservations taken.
restaurants_raw._merge_note: Same as Bar Avila Tapas from src-james-dimitri

restaurants_raw.name: Versos Sueltos
restaurants_raw.quote: Contemporary market cuisine in an intimate setting; €49 per person, online reservations required.
restaurants_raw._quality_flag: borderline: secondary source with only price + cuisine type as details

restaurants_raw.name: Caféteria d'Sano
restaurants_raw.quote: Tostadas (toasted bread with toppings) at this Cathedral-area breakfast spot; under €10 per person.
restaurants_raw._quality_flag: TIER-C: café/breakfast only + only 1 specific detail for secondary
